expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model with at least two parts (cam fixed to ground, follower)
expectations.evidence: model create model_name=model; part create rigid_body .model.cam and .model.follower both present

expectations.text: translational_joint: A translational joint constrains the follower to translate along X
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint translational joint_name=.model.follower_trans with ZXZ-oriented markers (90D,90D,0D) aligning local z with global +X

expectations.text: uses_step5_not_step: STEP5 is used for the cam force (not plain STEP)
expectations.evidence: function = "STEP5(DX(.model.follower.tip_mkr,.model.cam.ref_mkr),0.0,0.0,20.0,500.0)" â€” STEP5 explicitly used

expectations.text: correct_dx_range_0_to_20: STEP5 x-range spans 0 to 20
expectations.evidence: STEP5 arguments are x1=0.0, x2=20.0 matching required range

expectations.text: correct_force_range_0_to_500: STEP5 output range spans 0 to 500
expectations.evidence: STEP5 arguments are h1=0.0, h2=500.0 giving 0â€“500 N output range

expectations.text: hold_beyond_20mm: Force holds at 500 N for DX > 20 mm
expectations.evidence: STEP5 inherently returns h2=500.0 for any x > x2=20.0; confirmed by function definition

expectations.text: velocity_damping_2_vx: Damping term 2.0 * VX(...) added to force expression
expectations.evidence: Second string in function continuation: " - 2.0*VX(.model.follower.tip_mkr,.model.cam.ref_mkr)" present

expectations.text: quoted_expression: The FUNCTION= value is expressed as one or more double-quoted strings (comma-separated quoted strings are valid Adams CMD syntax for splitting long expressions and should be marked TRUE)
expectations.evidence: Expression split into two comma-separated quoted strings: "STEP5(...)" , " - 2.0*VX(...)" â€” this is correct Adams CMD syntax for multi-part function strings

expectations.text: geometry_present: At least one geometry shape added to each part
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder .model.cam.cam_cyl (r=40, l=30) and .model.follower.follower_cyl (r=10, l=60) both present

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All markers, parts, joints and forces use .model.part.object naming throughout (e.g. .model.follower.tip_mkr, .model.cam_ground_fix)

expectations.text: no_adams_id: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: No occurrence of adams_id found anywhere in the CMD file

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient type=auto_select end_time=1.0 number_of_steps=1000 model_name=.model

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exited 0; log shows 'batch run is finished'; no Adams ERROR: lines â€” only Python excepthook TypeError/SyntaxError at tail which are unrelated to the Adams model

expectations.text: uses_impact: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: IMPACT(...) is used explicitly in both the standalone expression block and the force_vector command.

expectations.text: correct_displacement_arg: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
expectations.evidence: First argument is DZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), measuring Z-displacement of CM relative to the floor reference marker.

expectations.text: correct_velocity_arg: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
expectations.evidence: Second argument is VZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), matching the DZ reference frame.

expectations.text: threshold_at_zero: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
expectations.evidence: Third argument (trigger/threshold) is 0.0; table confirms 'Contact activates when DZ < 0'.

expectations.text: correct_params: Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
expectations.evidence: Parameters are 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1 â€” all four correct values present in the IMPACT call.

expectations.text: quoted_expression: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: In the force_vector command, function_z segments are all wrapped in double quotes; function_x and function_y use "0.0".

expectations.text: fixes_model_create_abbreviation: 'model cre' expanded to 'model create'
expectations.evidence: Line 5: 'model create model_name = pendulum' â€” full keyword used

expectations.text: fixes_part_create_abbreviation: 'part cre rig nam' expanded to 'part create rigid_body name_and_position'
expectations.evidence: Line 33: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position &' â€” fully expanded

expectations.text: changes_mass_properties_create_to_modify: 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' changed to 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties'
expectations.evidence: Line 46: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties &' â€” uses modify, not create

expectations.text: removes_adams_id: 'adams_id = 3' removed
expectations.evidence: No 'adams_id' keyword appears anywhere in the output file

expectations.text: keeps_center_of_mass_marker_cm_created_first: The .pendulum.link.cm marker is explicitly created BEFORE the part modify mass_properties call, and center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm is kept in that call.
expectations.evidence: Lines 40-43: '.pendulum.link.cm' marker created at location 0,-100,0 before mass_properties block at line 46; line 50 includes 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm'

expectations.text: fixes_missing_dot_paths: 'pendulum.link.pin_mkr' corrected to '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr' in both marker create and i_marker_name
expectations.evidence: All marker_name and i_marker_name references use leading dot: '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr'

expectations.text: fixes_spring_damper_keyword: 'force create element_like spring_damper' -> 'translational_spring_damper' and 'length = 200.0' -> 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0'
expectations.evidence: Line 91: 'force create element_like translational_spring_damper &'; line 97: 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0'

expectations.text: fixes_simulation_command: 'sim trans end_time = 2.0 step_size = 0.001' -> 'simulation single_run transient' with proper parameters
expectations.evidence: Line 100: 'simulation single_run transient &' with type, end_time=2.0, number_of_steps=2000, model_name, initial_static=no

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: The corrected CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: No 'ERROR:' lines found in aview.log (only Python exception class names 'TypeError:' and 'SyntaxError:' from an unrelated internal traceback, not Adams ERROR: messages)

expectations.text: model_created: Script creates a model with model create command
expectations.evidence: Line: 'model create model_name = four_bar'

expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body name_and_position for .four_bar.crank, .four_bar.coupler, .four_bar.rocker

expectations.text: four_revolute_joints: Four revolute joints are created (crank-ground, crank-coupler, coupler-rocker, rocker-ground)
expectations.evidence: Four joints: rev_crank_ground, rev_crank_coupler, rev_coupler_rocker, rev_rocker_ground

expectations.text: motion_generator_on_crank: A motion generator drives the crank joint
expectations.evidence: constraint create motion_generator with joint_name = .four_bar.rev_crank_ground

expectations.text: constant_speed_360: Motion generator function specifies 360 degrees per second
expectations.evidence: function = "360D" with type_of_freedom = rotational and time_derivative = velocity; comment confirms 360 deg/s = 2*PI rad/s

expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient with end_time = 2.0, number_of_steps = 2000

expectations.text: creates_cm_and_passes_center_of_mass_marker: A .cm marker is explicitly created on each part BEFORE part modify mass_properties is called, and center_of_mass_marker is passed in every part modify rigid_body mass_properties call â€” if center_of_mass_marker is absent from ANY part modify call, this is FALSE
expectations.evidence: .four_bar.crank.cm, .four_bar.coupler.cm, .four_bar.rocker.cm each created before their respective part modify calls; center_of_mass_marker specified in all three mass_properties calls

expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: 'model create model_name = crane' present; parts .crane.tower, .crane.boom, .crane.payload all created with 'part create rigid_body name_and_position'.

expectations.text: markers_use_eval_loc: Boom and payload markers use eval() with loc_global() or loc_relative_to() to compute positions
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip uses 'location = (eval(loc_global({200, 0, 0}, .crane.boom.cm)))'; mkr_payload_hang uses 'location = (eval(loc_relative_to({0, 0, 150}, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip)))'.

expectations.text: markers_use_ori_along_axis: Both mkr_boom_tip and mkr_payload_hang use eval with ori_along_axis() with full dot-path marker names in the arguments
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: '(eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.cm, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))'; mkr_payload_hang: '(eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))'. Both use full dot-path names.

expectations.text: eval_expressions_single_line: Every eval expression stays entirely on one line. If any eval(...) is split across a line continuation (&), this is FALSE.
expectations.evidence: All four eval calls (loc_global, ori_along_axis x2, loc_relative_to) are each written on a single line with no & continuation inside the eval().

expectations.text: function_string_wrapped: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines using comma-separated quoted strings rather than being on one enormous single line
expectations.evidence: FUNCTION= value is broken into 14 comma-separated quoted string segments across multiple continuation lines ("STEP5(TIME,...)", " + STEP5(...)", etc.).

expectations.text: joints_correct: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom) are created
expectations.evidence: 'constraint create joint revolute' for .crane.jnt_tower_ground and .crane.jnt_boom_tower; 'constraint create joint spherical' for .crane.jnt_payload_boom.

expectations.text: force_expression_correct: The force function includes STEP5 ramps, a VZ velocity-damping term, and an IMPACT contact term
expectations.evidence: Function contains 'STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)' and 'STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)' ramps, '- 2.0 * VZ(...)' damping, and 'IMPACT(DZ(...), VZ(...), 0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)' contact.

expectations.text: mass_properties_uses_modify: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: All three parts (.crane.tower, .crane.boom, .crane.payload) use 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' with mass, ixx/iyy/izz, and center_of_mass_marker.

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names consistently use .crane.* prefix throughout the script (e.g., .crane.tower, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.jnt_tower_ground).

expectations.text: for_loop_used: A for / end loop drives mass and/or spring-damper creation
expectations.evidence: Two for/end loops: one iterating i=1..8 to create point masses, one iterating j=1..7 to create spring-dampers.

expectations.text: eval_function_used: EVAL() used to dereference a variable inside a command string
expectations.evidence: EVAL() used extensively, e.g. (eval(".chain_masses.mass_" // RTOI(i))), (eval((i - 1) * .chain_masses.spacing)), (eval(.chain_masses.stiffness)), etc.

expectations.text: rtoi_function_used: RTOI() used to convert the loop counter to a string for name construction
expectations.evidence: RTOI(i) and RTOI(j) used throughout both loops for name construction, e.g. RTOI(i), RTOI(j), RTOI(j + 1).

expectations.text: string_concat_operator_used: // string concatenation operator used to build part/marker names
expectations.evidence: // used in all name-building expressions, e.g. ".chain_masses.mass_" // RTOI(i) // ".cm", "sd_" // RTOI(j) // "_" // RTOI(j + 1).

expectations.text: length_param: Includes displacement_at_preload = 250 (NOT 'length = 250' or 'free_length = 250')

expectations.text: marker_references: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: Both i_marker_name and j_marker_name are present in the syntax block and complete example (spring_i_mkr and spring_j_mkr).

expectations.text: explains_when_to_use: Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
expectations.evidence: Key Rules states 'The I and J markers define the line of action'; the force equation F = -K*(d-d0) - C*d_dot and use of a translational joint in the example make the 1-DOF along-a-line-of-action nature explicit.

expectations.text: uses_step5_not_step: STEP5 is used for the cam force (not plain STEP)

expectations.evidence: STEP5 arguments: y1=0.0, y2=500.0

expectations.text: hold_beyond_20mm: Force holds at 500 N for DX > 20 mm

expectations.text: quoted_expression: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: CMD block shows: function = "IMPACT(...)" â€” expression is double-quoted.

expectations.text: damping_param: Includes damping = 100
expectations.evidence: Both examples include 'damping = 100.0'

expectations.text: length_param: Includes displacement_at_preload = 250 (NOT 'length = 250' or 'free_length = 250' â€” must be displacement_at_preload)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'free_length = 250.0' throughout â€” the required parameter name 'displacement_at_preload' is never used

expectations.text: marker_references: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: Both 'i_marker_name' and 'j_marker_name' are present in the spring-damper command block

expectations.text: explains_when_to_use: Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
expectations.evidence: States 'The force acts along the line joining the two markers (the I–J axis)' and Key Rules note 'Adams always measures the spring force along the instantaneous I–J vector'
